frame_analyses.description: Wire chaser task setup shown on screen title. FLS trainer visible with two wire paths (one at top, one at bottom) suspended between alligator clips. Left and right laparoscopic instruments (Maryland dissector and scissors) positioned at workspace.

frame_analyses.technique_notes: Wire chasing technique, not pattern cutting.

frame_analyses.description: Wire dissection continuing. Right instrument (scissors) tracking along wire path. Wire remains intact and suspended.

Right scissors continuing dissection along lower wire. Left dissector stabilizing tissue.
Wire nearly fully exposed from trainer material. Scissors at the distal end of lower wire path.

frame_analyses.description: Wire chasing task complete. Both wire paths fully exposed and freed from trainer material. Instruments positioned at completion.

task_specific_assessments.critical_issue: TASK MISMATCH - Video shows Wire Chaser task demonstration, NOT Pattern Cut (task2_pattern_cut). No circular pattern, no gauze, no cutting task present. No pattern cut scoring applicable.

confidence_rationale: Video is definitively titled 'WIRE CHASER' with on-screen attribution to Masie Rahimi MD, Amsterdam UMC. The apparatus, tools, and task structure all match Wire Chaser (FLS task 4 or similar advanced task), not Pattern Cut (task2). No 4cm marked circle, no gauze membrane, no cutting action. Task mismatch is 100% certain.

technique_summary: This is an expert-level demonstration of the Wire Chaser task, showing clean dissection technique with smooth instrument coordination. However, it is NOT the requested Pattern Cut task (task2_pattern_cut).
strengths: Expert dissection technique with minimal unnecessary movement
Good instrument coordination between left and right hands
Clean wire separation without tearing or damage

demonstrated_techniques: Maryland dissector for tissue retraction and dissection
Scissors for fine cutting and wire isolation
Wire tracking and dissection from surrounding tissue
common_errors_addressed: Preventing excessive tearing of surrounding trainer material
Maintaining visibility of wire path during dissection
